reasons: Valir can deny Dyrroth's preferred short-range burst by pushing him away.
Dyrroth is much less threatening when he spends his dash but cannot stay attached to the target.

proof.summary: Dyrroth wants to dash in and force a melee burst trade, while Valir can knock him back, slow him, and create space.
worksBestWhen: Valir saves Searing Torrent for Dyrroth's commit.
Valir keeps minion and frontline spacing so Dyrroth cannot dash freely.
failureCases: Dyrroth flanks and reaches Valir before knockback is available.
Valir wastes disengage on a fake engage.

reasons: Minsitthar can punish Dyrroth's dash-reliant engage by denying blink or dash movement inside his ultimate zone.
This turns Dyrroth's all-in into a trapped fight if the team follows up.

proof.summary: Dyrroth needs Spectre Step to enter and reposition, while Minsitthar's King's Calling can disable dash or blink skills in the fight area.
worksBestWhen: Minsitthar casts ultimate where Dyrroth must commit.
Allies focus Dyrroth while his mobility is restricted.
failureCases: Dyrroth baits King's Calling and disengages.
Minsitthar casts too late after Dyrroth has already finished his burst.

reasons: Ruby can disrupt Dyrroth's melee rhythm with repeated pull and stun control.
Her sustain and control make it harder for Dyrroth to finish a clean one-combo duel.

proof.summary: Dyrroth wants an uninterrupted melee burst, while Ruby can stun, pull, and interrupt his positioning during the trade.
worksBestWhen: Ruby keeps enough spacing to avoid taking the full burst instantly.
Ruby fights with minions or allies nearby to extend her sustain window.
failureCases: Dyrroth lands full burst before Ruby can sustain.
Ruby uses control before Dyrroth commits.

reasons: Kaja can punish Dyrroth when he dives too far forward.
Suppression prevents Dyrroth from freely continuing his combo or escaping after entry.

proof.summary: Dyrroth wants to commit forward for burst, while Kaja can suppress and drag him into team follow-up.
worksBestWhen: Kaja waits until Dyrroth spends his dash.
The team is ready to burst during Divine Judgment.
failureCases: Dyrroth avoids Kaja and targets another side of the fight.
Kaja uses suppression before Dyrroth commits.

reasons: Pharsa can punish Dyrroth from outside his melee burst range.
Dyrroth has to find a flank or force cooldowns before he can threaten Pharsa.

proof.summary: Dyrroth wants short-range all-in trades, while Pharsa can pressure from long range and make him spend time crossing space.
worksBestWhen: Pharsa has vision around flank routes.
A frontline prevents Dyrroth from directly reaching her.
failureCases: Dyrroth reaches Pharsa during her channel.
Pharsa lacks vision and gets flanked.
